perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information disclosed by Discloser shall remain the property of such Discloser, and no license or other rights to such Discloser ’s Confidential Information is granted or implied hereby.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information disclosed by Discloser shall become the joint property of both Discloser and Recipient, granting each party equal rights to use and license it, unless otherwise agreed in writing.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 4 contradicts the original statement that Confidential Information remains solely the property of the Discloser. This creates ambiguity because Section 4 initially grants no implied rights, whereas the modified version grants immediate, equal rights to the Recipient, potentially leading to disputes over ownership and usage of the Confidential Information. This alteration specifically contradicts the initial principle of maintaining the Discloser's exclusive ownership and control over the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership and Return of Confidential Information . Confidential Information disclosed by Discloser shall become the joint property of both Discloser and Recipient, granting each party equal rights to use and license it, unless otherwise agreed in writing. Recipient shall reproduce the symbols, legends or other p roprietary notices affixed to Confidential Information, and shall not, nor permit any third party to, remove, add or modify the same . Recipient shall, upon termination of this Agreement, or upon written request of Discloser, whichever is earlier, immediately, but not later than 10 days after any notice thereof by Discloser, return (or destroy at Discloser’s option) all copies of such Discloser’s Confidential Information and certify in writing its compliance with this requirement, except that Recipient may retain a copy of such Confidential Information solely for archival purpose .
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4 directly contradicts the original agreement that Confidential Information remains the sole property of the Discloser. The modified version grants equal rights to both Discloser and Recipient, which is a clear contradiction. The contradicted text also states that Confidential Information disclosed by Discloser shall become the joint property of both Discloser and Recipient, granting each party equal rights to use and license it, unless otherwise agreed in writing. This directly conflicts with the initial principle of maintaining the Discloser's exclusive ownership and control over the confidential information, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient’s duty to protect Discloser’s Confidential Information expires 5 years from the date on which that Confidential Information was disclosed to Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient’s duty to protect Discloser’s Confidential Information continues indefinitely, regardless of the termination of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 8 removes the time limit on the Recipient's confidentiality obligations. This contradicts the original five-year limit, leading to uncertainty about the duration of confidentiality duties. If another clause in the agreement refers to the original 5-year expiration, this change introduces a direct conflict regarding how long the Recipient must protect the Confidential Information. This indefinite duty of protection could create in-text contradictions with other sections outlining termination conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term and Termination . This Agreement shall be effective from the Effective Date until 24 months thereafter. Either party may terminate this Agreement for any or no reason upon 10 days written notice to the other party. However, any termination of this Agreement shall not relieve Recipient of its confidentiality and use obligations with respec t to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the date of such termination. Except for the right to use Confidential Information for the Purpose, which right terminates when this Agreement terminates, Recipient’s duty to protect Discloser’s Confidential Information continues indefinitely, regardless of the termination of this agreement. Sections 4, 5, 7, 8, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 and 16 shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the confidentiality obligation to 5 years. The changed text makes the confidentiality obligation indefinite, regardless of termination. The contradicted text, which is also the changed text, states that the Recipient’s duty to protect Discloser’s Confidential Information continues indefinitely, regardless of the termination of this agreement. This directly contradicts the original 5-year limit, creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding the duration of the confidentiality obligation. The change removes a time limit that was previously in place, creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: All disputes, controversies or claims between the parties arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (including its existence, validity or termination) shall be finally resolved by arbitration to be held in Seoul, Korea and conducted in English under the Rules of Arbitration of the Intern ational Chamber of Commerce ; provided, however, that each party may enforce its or its affiliates’ intellectual property rights in any court of competent jurisdiction, including but not limit ed to equitable relief .
perturbation.changed_text: All disputes, controversies or claims between the parties arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (including its existence, validity or termination) may be resolved through mediation in a location mutually agreed upon by both parties. If mediation fails, either party may pursue litigation in the courts of their respective jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 15 replaces mandatory arbitration with optional mediation, and if that fails, allows litigation in either party's jurisdiction. This directly contradicts the original clause requiring arbitration in Seoul, Korea. This could lead to disputes over the proper forum for resolving disputes under the agreement, as one party could argue for arbitration based on the original language, while the other argues for mediation and potential litigation based on the altered language. The specific contradiction arises in how disputes are to be resolved, changing from a mandatory, predefined method to a flexible, potentially litigious approach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Law and Jurisdiction . This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Korea (excluding conflict of laws provisions which may direct the application of another jurisdiction’s laws). All disputes, controversies or claims between the parties arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (including its existence, validity or termination) may be resolved through mediation in a location mutually agreed upon by both parties. If mediation fails, either party may pursue litigation in the courts of their respective jurisdiction. The arbitral award shall be final and binding on the parties. Except to the extent entry of judgment and any subsequent enforcement may require disclosure, all matters relating to the arbitration, including the award, shall be held in confidence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces mandatory arbitration in Seoul, Korea, with optional mediation and potential litigation in either party's jurisdiction. The contradicted text also includes the changed text, which now contradicts the original clause requiring arbitration in Seoul, Korea, and stating that the arbitral award shall be final and binding. This creates a direct conflict regarding the dispute resolution mechanism, making it unclear whether disputes should be arbitrated or litigated, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and may not be amended or modified except in writing signed by each of the parties to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the initial understanding between the parties; subsequent agreements, whether written or oral, may supersede the terms outlined herein.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 16 allows for subsequent oral or written agreements to supersede the original agreement, directly contradicting the original clause that required amendments to be in writing and signed by both parties. This ambiguity undermines the enforceability of the entire agreement, as either party could argue that a later, even unwritten, agreement modified the terms. This creates an in-text contradiction to the intire agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement . This Agreement constitutes the initial understanding between the parties; subsequent agreements, whether written or oral, may supersede the terms outlined herein. The English language text of this Agreement shall prevail over any translations thereof. If a contract is entered into by the parties in respect of the Purpose, this Agreement shall be deemed incorporated into such contract in addition to any confidential ity obligations set forth in such contract, if any , and the term of this Agreement is deemed to be extended (but not shorted) to be the same as that of the contract .
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement could only be amended in writing signed by both parties. The changed text allows for subsequent agreements, whether written or oral, to supersede the original terms. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which means that the changed text directly contradicts the original agreement, creating an in-text contradiction as it allows for oral agreements to modify the original written agreement, undermining its enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information . “Confidential Information” includes a ll business, financial, contractual, marketing and/or technical information, in whatever form embodied, which has been or may be disclosed , or to which access is provided, by a party (“Discloser”) to the other party to this Agreement (“Recipient”) , which (a) if in wri ting, is marked “confidential”, “proprietary” or other similar marking at the time of disclosure , or (b) if provided orally or visually, is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure and confirmed in writing to Recipient within 15 days of such disclosure .
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information . “Confidential Information” includes only technical information directly related to the KNOX Partnership, provided it is explicitly marked as "Confidential - KNOX" at the time of disclosure, regardless of the form in which it is embodied.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 1 significantly narrows the scope of what constitutes "Confidential Information." This creates a conflict because the original definition was broad, encompassing various types of information. The modified definition restricts it to only technical information related to the KNOX Partnership and requires a specific marking. The term will now have multiple interpretation throughout the agreement, creating in-text contradictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information . “Confidential Information” includes only technical information directly related to the KNOX Partnership, provided it is explicitly marked as "Confidential - KNOX" at the time of disclosure, regardless of the form in which it is embodied. Recipient may disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information to Recipient’s affiliates on the condition that Recipient shall restrict access to Discloser’s Confidential Information to those of Recipient’s affiliates’ officers, directors and employees who have a legitimate need -to-know to carry out the Purpose and who are obligated to protect such Confidential Information pursuant to terms and conditions no less protective of Discloser than those contained in this Agreement and Recipient shall be liable for any failure of its affiliates to abide by the provisions of this Agreement as if such failure was the act or omiss ion of such party. For the purposes of this Agreement, “affiliate ” means an entity that , as of Effective Date , directly or indirectly, controls or is under common control with a party to this Agreement, but only for so long as such control exists , and where “control” shall mean ownership of more than 50% of the stock or other equity interests entitled to vote for the election of directors or an equivalent governing body.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly, encompassing various types of business, financial, contractual, marketing, and technical information. The changed text narrows this definition to only technical information directly related to the KNOX Partnership and requires a specific marking ('Confidential - KNOX'). The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, now conflicts with the original intent of the agreement, as the definition of 'Confidential Information' has been significantly restricted. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement may rely on the broader definition of 'Confidential Information' in other sections, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability. The change introduces ambiguity where the original definition provided certainty, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall, upon termination of this Agreement, or upon written request of Discloser, whichever is earlier, immediately, but not later than 10 days after any notice thereof by Discloser, return (or destroy at Discloser’s option) all copies of such Discloser’s Confidential Information and certify in writing its compliance with this requirement, except that Recipient may retain a copy of such Confidential Information solely for archival purpose .
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall, upon termination of this Agreement, or upon written request of Discloser, whichever is earlier, make reasonable efforts to return or destroy Confidential Information within a timeframe deemed appropriate by the Recipient, and may retain copies indefinitely without providing certification.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces significant ambiguity by replacing the mandatory 'shall' with the subjective 'make reasonable efforts,' and removing the strict 10-day deadline and certification requirement. Now the term in the section will allow the reciepient to have complete flexibility over the data, creating an in-text contracition to the strict regulations in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership and Return of Confidential Information . Confidential Information disclosed by Discloser shall remain the property of such Discloser, and no license or other rights to such Discloser ’s Confidential Information is granted or implied hereby. Recipient shall reproduce the symbols, legends or other p roprietary notices affixed to Confidential Information, and shall not, nor permit any third party to, remove, add or modify the same . Recipient shall, upon termination of this Agreement, or upon written request of Discloser, whichever is earlier, make reasonable efforts to return or destroy Confidential Information within a timeframe deemed appropriate by the Recipient, and may retain copies indefinitely without providing certification. 5. No Reverse Engineering . No party, as Recipient, will decompile, disassemble, reverse engineer or attempt to reconst ruct, identify or discover any source code, underlying ideas, techniques or algorithms in Confidential Information by any means whatever, except as may be specifically authorized in advance by Discloser in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the mandatory 'shall' with the subjective 'make reasonable efforts,' removes the strict 10-day deadline, and eliminates the certification requirement. This directly contradicts the original intent of the 'Ownership and Return of Confidential Information' section, which aims to maintain strict control and ownership of confidential information by the Discloser. The changed text allows the Recipient complete flexibility over the data, creating a direct conflict with the Discloser's ownership rights and the requirement to return or destroy the information promptly. The contradicted text is also in the same location as the changed text, further highlighting the contradiction.
